name: From Dos Aguas to Quesa via Puerto de Millares
name_original: Desde Dos Aguas hasta Quesa por el Puerto de Millares
region_intro: Road cycling around Dos Aguas, located in the inland province of Valencia, Spain, offers varied terrain suitable for different fitness levels. The region is characterized by mountain passes, such as the Dos Aguas Pass and Fuente del Real, providing challenging ascents and descents. Riders can experience expansive views of Valencian orchards, valleys, and surrounding mountains. Routes often traverse rural scenery, including olive, carob, and almond groves, on well-paved roads. Best road cycling routes around Dos Aguas The most popular road cycling route is Jucar Canyons Trail from Cortes de Pallas, a 36.9 miles (59.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through canyon landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Dos Aguas to Quesa via Puerto de Millares, a difficult 51.9 miles (83.5 km) path. This route includes demanding ascents over mountain passes, offering panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Riu Magre - Pas de l'Horteta – Weir of the Magre River loop from Punta Galán, a 29.7 miles (47.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and rural scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Dos Aguas is defined by mountain passes, rural groves, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Dos Aguas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Dos Aguas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Dos Aguas?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Dos Aguas, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 900 times, rating them highly for their varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Dos Aguas?
A: Dos Aguas is known for its varied terrain, featuring significant mountain passes like the Dos Aguas Pass and Fuente del Real, which provide challenging ascents and descents. You'll also find routes through beautiful rural scenery with olive, carob, and almond groves, often on well-paved roads. Many routes offer expansive views of Valencian orchards, valleys, and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for beginners or less experienced cyclists in Dos Aguas?
A: Yes, while the region is known for challenging ascents, there are 5 easy and 21 moderate road cycling routes available, making it accessible for less experienced cyclists. These routes often traverse river valleys and rural landscapes.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Dos Aguas?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Dos Aguas offers 16 difficult routes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839605913/jucar-canyons-trail-from-cortes-de-pallas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jucar Canyons Trail from Cortes de Pallas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e857213220/from-dos-aguas-to-quesa-via-puerto-de-millares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Dos Aguas to Quesa via Puerto de Millares&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and demanding ascents over mountain passes.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer panoramic views from mountain passes, such as the Dos Aguas Pass, which acts as a 'balcony' overlooking Valencian orchards. You can also find routes that pass by the ruins of Madrona Castle on the banks of the River Júcar. For specific viewpoints, consider routes that include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pileta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Dos Aguas area?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40020202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riu Magre - Pas de l'Horteta – Weir of the Magre River loop from Punta Galán&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 47.8 km trail through river valleys and rural scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Dos Aguas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes with panoramic views to serene rides through rural groves, all on well-paved roads.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I explore near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, you can explore historic sites such as the ruins of Dos Aguas Castle and the Vilaragut Tower, both medieval fortifications offering impressive views. The Nuestra Señora del Rosario Parish Church and the public wash house in Dos Aguas village are also points of interest. The ruins of Madrona Castle are also visible along the River Júcar.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes accessible from the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region features several natural water attractions. You might find routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mañán Pool&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6610287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall at Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6796199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca de las Palomas&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/331736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turche Cave Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Dos Aguas?
A: Dos Aguas offers a pleasant climate for cycling for much of the year. While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's inland Valencia location generally means mild winters and warm, dry summers, making spring and autumn ideal for comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there any very long-distance road cycling routes for endurance riders?
A: Yes, for endurance riders, there are routes that extend significantly. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33847186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint John the Baptist loop from Dos Aguas&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 156 km with substantial elevation gain.
tips: One of the most picturesque and peaceful passes in the Valencian Community. From the village of Dos Aguas, the climb is approximately 360 meters over 11 kilometers, making it very easy to cycle. The views are breathtaking throughout the entire route.
